message:
perf(raptorq): fused sparse elimination, pivot scaling helper, and decoder API simplification

Three targeted optimizations to the RaptorQ decoding pipeline:

- decoder.rs: Rewrite sparse_update_columns_for_elimination_if_beneficial_into()
  to fuse the "is this row sparse?" check with "collect non-pivot columns"
  in a single pass, eliminating a redundant scan and O(n) vector remove.
  Extract scale_pivot_row_and_rhs() helper consolidating repeated
  pivot-row scaling with a fast-path when inverse is GF(256) ONE.

- linalg.rs: Add factor_is_one fast-path in GaussianSolver::eliminate_row()
  that uses gf256_add_slice (XOR only) instead of gf256_addmul_slice
  (multiply-then-XOR) when the elimination factor is ONE, avoiding
  unnecessary GF(256) multiplications.

- decoding.rs: Simplify decode_block() to use decoder's constraint_symbols()
  method instead of manually building constraint rows from ConstraintMatrix.
  Source and padding symbols now use identity-matrix columns directly rather
  than looking up the full constraint matrix, and decoded output uses the
  decoder's result.source field instead of manual intermediate reconstruction.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
